region_of_origin: Singapore (served at Potato Corner, a Filipino-origin franchise)
food_subgroup: Battered and fried seafood with seasoned fries combo

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a combined prawn pops and seasoned fries combo meal. FOODON_03315091 (fried shrimp food product) is used as the closest parent for the seafood component. The fries component is not separately coded here.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Shrimps and prawns, cooked) is the closest parent for the prawn component. No composite combo code exists in FoodEx2 for this specific product. The fries component would map separately to potato products.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 (Fried food) is used as the closest parent category. No specific SNOMED CT code exists for prawn pops and fries combo.

serving.description: 1 combo (prawn pops portion + large fries portion)
serving.natural_language_portion: One Tera Prawn Pops Combo serving consists of approximately 380 g total, comprising a portion of battered deep-fried prawn pops and a large serving of seasoned flavoured fries, as served at Potato Corner Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Shrimp, breaded and fried (SR Legacy 15151) and Potato, french fries, fast food (SR Legacy 21142); composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct nutritional entry exists for the Tera Prawn Pops Combo as a branded product in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Values are estimated as a weighted composite: approximately 40% battered fried shrimp (USDA SR Legacy 15151, breaded fried shrimp) and 60% seasoned fast-food french fries (USDA SR Legacy 21142, fast-food french fries). The seasoning powder contribution to sodium is accounted for by applying a 15% upward adjustment to the fries sodium baseline, consistent with flavoured fries products. Per-100g values are derived from this blended composite. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — French fries / fried potato GI reference; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Tera Prawn Pops Combo. GI is estimated as a weighted composite: french fries GI ~63 (medium; University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2021, food ID 1546) weighted at 60% of carbohydrate contribution, and battered fried shrimp GI ~40 (low; analogous to battered seafood, estimated from closest parent in University of Sydney GI Database) weighted at 40%. Weighted composite GI = (63 × 0.60) + (40 × 0.40) = 37.8 + 16.0 = 53.8, rounded to 54. However, the dominant carbohydrate source is the seasoned fries, and the overall product is classified medium GI. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (63 × 127.3) / 100 = 80.2, rounded to 80.4, using fries-dominant GI as the primary driver per standard GL calculation convention for mixed meals. Serving carbohydrate = 33.5 g/100g × 380 g serving = 127.3 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from seasoning powder and batter
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 480 mg sodium per 100 g (equating to ~1,824 mg per 380 g combo serving), this meal provides over 79% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg in a single sitting, making it a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension who should limit or avoid this item.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate load and medium-to-high glycemic index from deep-fried seasoned fries
type2_diabetes.guidance: The combo delivers approximately 127 g of carbohydrates per serving with a medium GI (~63 for the fries component), resulting in a high glycemic load that will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this combination and pair with low-GI foods if consumed.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat, saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and sodium from deep-frying and seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The deep-fried preparation contributes approximately 14.8 g total fat and 2.6 g saturated fat per 100 g, with trace trans fats and 68 mg cholesterol, alongside very high sodium; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence and limit portion size.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Tera Prawn Pops Combo is a fast-food combo meal sold at Potato Corner outlets in Singapore, consisting of a large portion of battered deep-fried prawn pops paired with a generous serving of seasoned flavoured fries. The prawn pops are bite-sized prawns encased in a light crispy batter and deep-fried until golden, offering a crunchy exterior with a juicy, tender prawn interior. The fries are Potato Corner's signature seasoned fries, available in a variety of flavour powders such as cheese, barbecue, sour cream, or chilli, giving them a bold, savoury, and sometimes tangy taste profile. The combo is classified as ultra-processed due to the deep-frying method, use of batter, and application of flavoured seasoning powders. Nutritionally, this is a calorie-dense meal at approximately 295 kcal per 100 g, with a full 380 g serving delivering around 1,121 kcal. It is high in carbohydrates (~127 g per serving), moderate-to-high in fat (~56 g per serving), and provides meaningful protein (~31 g per serving) from the prawn component. Sodium content is very high at ~1,824 mg per serving, raising significant concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. The glycemic load is high due to the large fries portion, making this a high-concern food for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The combo is popular as a snack or light meal in Singapore's hawker and mall food court culture, and is associated with Western-style fast food and snack cuisine. It contains crustacean allergens (prawns), gluten (batter and seasoning), and potential dairy traces in seasoning powders. It is generally considered halal-friendly at Potato Corner Singapore outlets.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists for the Tera Prawn Pops Combo as a branded product in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA Branded Foods. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for breaded fried shrimp and fast-food french fries. GI is estimated from analogous foods in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach and the absence of a direct branded product match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA SR Legacy 15151 (breaded fried shrimp, ~40% weight contribution) and USDA SR Legacy 21142 (fast-food french fries, ~60% weight contribution), with a 15% sodium upward adjustment for flavoured seasoning powder. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimation. 3. Trans fat is present at trace levels (0.1 g/100g) from deep-frying oil; this is a low but non-zero value consistent with commercial deep-frying. 4. GI is estimated at 63 (medium) using the fries-dominant component from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2021); the composite weighted GI is approximately 54 but the fries-dominant GI of 63 is used for glycemic load calculation as the primary carbohydrate driver. 5. Serving size of 380 g is an estimate for the Tera (larger) combo size based on typical fast-food combo portion weights; actual weight may vary by outlet. 6. Halal status is based on general Potato Corner Singapore outlet practice; individual outlet certification should be verified. 7. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from the USDA SR Legacy composite and are not confirmed from a direct branded product analysis. 8. The food_name has been normalised from the field survey menu name 'Tera Prawn Pops Combo' to the canonical English descriptor 'Prawn Pops and Seasoned Fries Combo'; the original menu name is preserved in aliases.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed. Identified that food_name needed normalisation from menu name to canonical English descriptor. Multilingual names added for all four mandatory Singaporean languages. Nutrient values estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food.

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list — all entries confirmed present verbatim. Verified vitamin_d_mcg -1 sentinel value documented in notes. Verified all ontology match_type values are from allowed set only. Verified zero-macro justification not needed as no core macro is zero. Confirmed g3 numeric hygiene: all per_100g values are numeric, -1 used only for vitamin_d_mcg with documentation. Final self-score: 8/8, all hard gates pass. Decision: PASS.
